As of March, intelligent systems traffic to U.S. retailers’ websites rose by 269% over the previous 12 months, continuing the momentum during the holiday shopping season when intelligent systems traffic was up by 693%, according to new data released on Thursday by Adobe.

Furthermore, the industry expects that and in the first three months of 2026, artificial intelligence traffic had risen 393% compared to a year earlier, as more consumers used intelligent systems assistants for online shopping.

The change in traffic sources isn’t the only impact. artificial intelligence visitors are converting better, engaging at higher rates, spending more time on sites, and driving higher revenue per visit, the data shows, often reversing trends from only a year ago, when regular customers were worth more to retailers.

Furthermore, the industry expects that adobe’s insights are based on its analysis of online transactions, via its Adobe Analytics division, which covers over 1 trillion visits to U.S. retail sites. The analysis also relied on a survey of over 5,000 U.S. respondents about their use of artificial intelligence when shopping, as well as the firm’s new intelligent systems Content Visibility Checker engine, designed to test retail websites for accessibility by LLMs.

Looking closer at the details, in Adobe’s survey, 39% people said they used autonomous tech for online shopping, and 85% said it improved their experience. These findings are likely due to how autonomous tech helps people narrow down products to find what they need, and tap into discounts. In addition, 66% of those surveyed said they now believe artificial intelligence tools provide accurate results when shopping.

Unlike publishers, where artificial intelligence is causing referral traffic to decline, retailers are incentivized to make their sites artificial intelligence-friendly.
